title: Howardia caudata
taxonomy: Howardia caudata belongs to the family Boraginaceae. It is a genus of plants that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The Boraginaceae family is a large group of flowering plants that includes over 2,000 species. Howardia caudata is further classified as a part of the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants like mint and honeysuckle. Within the family Boraginaceae, Howardia caudata is also part of the tribe Lithospermeae.

intro: Howardia caudata, also known as Howardia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat various ailments including skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Howardia caudata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Howardia is commonly prepared as a tincture or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to support skin health and immune function.
